rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. RICHARD KIWATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To List Receivership Property; Request For Increase In Receivership Certificate; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Gerard F. Keena, Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2020 line 1. OTHER GERARD KEENA II Notice Of Motion And Motion To List Receivership Property; Request For Increase In Receivership Certificate Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Receiver's Motion for Leave to List Receivership Property is GRANTED. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2020 line 4. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Amended Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. rulings.Case Title: DORAN CONSTRUCTION & DESIGN INC. VS. ROBERT POYNTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action, And For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT ROBERT POYNTER, NILI POYNTER Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Notice Of Pendency Of Action, And For Attorneys Fees is continued to August 26, 2020 for parties to submit supplemental briefing. By no later than August 12, 2020 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief addressing the following issues: (1) Can a second lis pendens be recorded while there is an allegedly valid lis pendens already on the record. Response format shall be "yes" or "no" with citation to authority followed; (2) Does the recordation of the second lis pendens withdraw the recordation of the first lis pendens. Response format shall be "yes" or "no" with citation to authority followed; (3) List evidence supporting each element of partition cause of action submitted with the opposition by reference the document and page/paragraph number. Response should preferably be in a table format. Column one to state the element; Column two to list the evidence, e.g. Declaration of John Smith, paragraph 3 and/or exhibit 2, page 4, paragraph 3. Defendants may file a response to the supplemental brief (in the same format) no later than August 19, 2020. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JASON COLLINS VS. ADA MORALES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2020 line 9. DEFENDANT ADA MORALES, LAWRENCE MORALES Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Motion to Strike Portions of the Complaint is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to paragraph 18. Non-opposition filed. Motion is otherwise denied. Allegations in the complaint are sufficient to support the claims subject to this motion. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANTON WANG VS. ZHENG CHIU CHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 30, 2020 line 11. PLAINTIFF ANTON WANG MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Withdraw. Hearing Required to address whether withdrawal request by Suzan Yee of Tsao-Wu & Yee, LLP is effective to withdraw on behalf of Sean Makarin of Parseley & Makarin. If counsel intends to present authority on this issue supplemental brief (not to exceed 2 pages) to be filed and and served no later than 9:00 a.m. on July 30, 2020. Motion is GRANTED as to Suzan Yee and Tsao-Wu, LLP. =(501/CFH)
